amendments_note: spec 07 §1/§6.1: this run applies the degrade-to-uncapped amendment (ABC weights are properties of the shared posterior, applicable to any corridor under the same draws). The amendment itself rides N6; this artifact is its operational record.

assumptions_manifest.note: spec 07 §9 N4 registry conversion: the constant-tier registry leaves capcost + this harness consume, declared here so the assumptions registry claims each as a network-artifact row (check_assumptions' network scan; the sensitivity-block ids are harness-INTERNAL and exempt from the orphan check, mirroring the spec 08 §9 Q7 wrapper engine-owned precedent). The values_hash of these + the active prior bands enters the run_id preimage (D60 rec 3a). N5 review fix: per-candidate crossing COUNTS (spec 04 §3.3) are a candidate_universe field, not a registry constant -- only the per-crossing RATES (cap_crossing_low/ut) are registry leaves here; both feed capital_bands(), and the counts enter the run_id preimage separately as candidate_crossings (below), not the values_hash.

candidate_universe.substitution_note: spec 07 §4.3: the candidate pool is meant to come from the stage-1 DRM screen re-run against the updated network (gate 1). Until spec 01 lands, the candidate universe is HAND-SUPPLIED -- the 13-arterial HANDOFF shortlist is the natural seed. This N1b run supplies the TWO corridors that already have committed configs + derived files [harbor, streetcar], which is exactly the point: 2 candidates -> 2 singles + 2 directional pairs + trivial swaps at full N=40,000 CRN, small enough to prove the sequencing machinery end to end. More candidates arrive with stage 1. This substitution is surfaced in the primary artifact (network_sequence.json.candidate_universe.hand_supplied).

channel_split_note: channel_split (spec 07 N4) only ever appears on a candidate evaluated against a NONEMPTY network -- cycle 2 onward, 1-based (cycles[k].cycle == k, 0-based internally; see stopping_record.cycle_numbering); it has no meaning at cycle 1's empty network and is null there by construction. In THIS interim run every candidate is eventually committed (candidate_exhaustion, no budget bound), so a real, non-null channel_split DOES appear from cycle 2 onward (cycles[1].candidate_results) -- unlike the sibling NPV artifact, where the §7 marginal stop fires at cycle 1 before any commitment and every channel_split field is null.

archetype_gap.reason: archetypes are owner-designed complete networks with declared internal build orders (spec 07 §5.3 / §11 Q3); the harness must not generate them (a generator reintroduces the combinatorial problem this spec declines to solve). N3 supplies config/archetypes/*.json + the matched-capital gap series; until then the safeguard comparison line carries greedy vs best-single only.

cv.timing_split_note: interim: delta = 1 (welfare-minutes are a level, never discounted); the one-cycle_gap deferral shows only in the Delta-K_PV display
interim: delta = 1 (welfare-minutes are a level, never discounted); the one-cycle_gap deferral shows only in the Delta-K_PV display

capital_delta_K_pv.discount_note: Delta-K_PV display only (spec 07 §3); interim welfare is never discounted. rate 7% real, BCA-wrapper property, cited not owned

crossings.basis: spec 04 §3.3's named set for this corridor: Caltrans I-5, Caltrans SR-22, the Santa Ana River flood-control channel, and the BNSF Fullerton terminal approach -- 4 major freeway/river/railroad special structures (N5 review fix: previously priced at crossings=0, silently omitting this line).

commitment.rationale: harbor = argmax CV (own Delta-wm P50 163,549 + continuation 54,493 = CV 218,114).

welfare_per_dollar_ratio_DISPLAY_ONLY.label: INTERIM / DISPLAY ONLY -- never quoted as BCA output

interaction_matrix.tau_caveat: tau pinning (spec 07 §8a) pins transfer volume to a share of base boardings, so cross-line synergy in the objective is MUTED -- this interaction estimate is a lower bound on true complementarity; records-request item 3 is the eventual fix

swap_results.note: runner-up-only bounded swap (spec 07 §5.2); no committed HYPOTHETICAL line was displaced -- with 2 candidates the swap suffix is trivial (the runner-up is the sole alternative). Built (asbuilt) lines are never swapped.

archetype_gap.reason: archetypes are owner-designed complete networks with declared internal build orders (spec 07 §5.3 / §11 Q3); the harness must not generate them (a generator reintroduces the combinatorial problem this spec declines to solve). N3 supplies config/archetypes/*.json + the matched-capital gap series; until then the safeguard comparison line carries greedy vs best-single only.

channel_split.method: reviewer toggle (spec 07 N4): anchor-add-only vs rebuild-only vs both, common random numbers; the rebuild channel is synthetic-feeder MARKET ENLARGEMENT, not crossing complementarity

cv.timing_split_note: interim: delta = 1 (welfare-minutes are a level, never discounted); the one-cycle_gap deferral shows only in the Delta-K_PV display

capital_delta_K_pv.discount_note: Delta-K_PV display only (spec 07 §3); interim welfare is never discounted. rate 7% real, BCA-wrapper property, cited not owned

crossings.basis: Determined honestly from the real alignment, not assumed equal to harbor's count (N5 review fix). ONE major crossing counts under spec 04 §3.3's freeway/river/railroad categories: the Santa Ana River channel (~mi 2.8, near Raitt St) -- OCTA's own project description has the line 'cross over the Santa Ana River ... on bridges' (a new double-track structure paralleling the inadequate single-track PE-era bridge), the same flood-control-authority category as harbor's river crossing. NOT counted: I-5 (geometry check against OSM I-5 way geometry -- the corridor_waypoints polyline's longitude band, -117.857 to -117.920, stays entirely west of I-5's alignment near this latitude, -117.845 to -117.863; SARTC sits ~0.4-0.6 mi west of I-5 and the route heads further west from there, so it never crosses); SR-22 (runs at lat 33.766-33.779 per OSM, north of the corridor's max latitude 33.7609); and no BNSF/active-railroad special structure (the corridor's own OSM geometry shows exactly two dedicated bridges -- the river crossing above and a Westminster Ave. grade separation near the terminus -- and Westminster Ave is an ordinary arterial street, not one of §3.3's three named categories, so it is excluded from the count under the same category discipline as harbor's, even though the real project grade-separates it operationally).

commitment.rationale: streetcar = argmax CV (own Delta-wm P50 54,493 + continuation 0 = CV 54,493).

welfare_per_dollar_ratio_DISPLAY_ONLY.label: INTERIM / DISPLAY ONLY -- never quoted as BCA output

swap_results.note: runner-up-only bounded swap (spec 07 §5.2); no committed HYPOTHETICAL line was displaced -- with 2 candidates the swap suffix is trivial (the runner-up is the sole alternative). Built (asbuilt) lines are never swapped.

frontier.aggregation: within-draw (spec 07 §3): sum lines per draw, then percentiles
frontier.flyvbjerg_annotation: portfolio optimism is worse than single-project optimism -- forecast errors are correlated across the model's lines (spec 05 §4.3; within-draw aggregation plus the sigma_struct row make this arithmetic, not rhetoric).

sigma_struct_row.mechanism: harness-side per-line INDEPENDENT N(0, sigma_struct) noise on the boardings scale, converted to welfare-minutes by each line's welfare-per-boarding ratio and summed within-draw; seeded deterministically from the run fingerprint. NO new PRIORS key (the model rng stream is untouched) -- an append-last prior is not required because the error is post-processing, not a swept model input (spec 07 §3/§8g).
sigma_struct_row.note: the within-draw sum carries only the correlated parameter component; sigma_struct adds the per-line idiosyncratic structural error the portfolio bands otherwise omit -- they widen (P10 down, P90 up), the P50 is ~unchanged (mean-zero noise). This is the arithmetic behind the Flyvbjerg annotation.

governance_note: in-run commitments are RECOMMENDATIONS (spec 07 §1); spec 00 §3 gate discipline applies at each REAL programmatic commitment. Each line's forecast of record remains its own stage-3 STOPS run.

kernel_set.note: spec 07 §6.1: all candidates ranked under the SHARED OC posterior (the 543_launch_s500 ABC weights), uncapped alongside; identical across corridors under CRN, so computed once per cycle. ESS reported for every ABC-weighted statistic (spec 02 §4.4 ESS<1000 saturation rule).

objective.note: INTERIM (spec 07 §3): the full-NPV objective is N5, gated on R1 -> R6 -> W1. Delta-K shown as the spec 04 LOW | US-TYPICAL band; welfare-per-dollar ratio is a display column only.

provenance_report.dependency_predicate: depends = omega>0.01 OR co-located persistent injection OR feeder/transfer edge in the rebuilt inputs.
provenance_report.depth_rule: depth(measured)=0; depth(eval)=1+max{depth(H): H hypothetical in network-before AND candidate depends on H}; committed line inherits eval depth; cap 2 -> EXPLORATORY, excluded from gate memos (spec 07 §6.2).
provenance_report.flyvbjerg: network optimism > single-project optimism (correlated errors); the depth cap is the control (spec 07 §6.2).
provenance_report.operating_mode: a planning tool re-run between real build cycles as lines open and forecasts become measurements; provenance depth resets from the updated network_asbuilt each re-run (spec 07 §6.2).
provenance_report.run_id_preimage: run_id = sha256 of canonical{candidates, asbuilt lines, seed, n, scenario, budget, assumptions_values_hash}. D60 review rec 3a added assumptions_values_hash = sha256{consumed capital constants + harness knobs + active prior bands}; it MOVES the id whenever the rate card or a prior band changes. Consequence recorded for provenance: the id here DIFFERS from the committed N1b/D60 artifact (d8b4a016...), which was keyed on inputs only and did not track the forecast-moving 60-mph v_cruise recenter. No timestamps enter the preimage (gate G6).

safeguard_comparison.note: safeguard = max{greedy portfolio, best single feasible, best feasible archetype}; the archetype leg lands with N3.

sensitivity.base_objective_note: committed-order cumulative welfare-minutes P50 (final portfolio, within-draw)

computed_n1b.note: cycle_gap is Delta-K_PV display only; welfare objective invariant (interim: level, not discounted)
cycle_gap is Delta-K_PV display only; welfare objective invariant (interim: level, not discounted)
omega walk-bin-mass allocation (spec 07 N4 margin-distribution variant: H's margin allocated by its measured walk-access mass profile at each tract's nearest-stop distance, §4.2)

spec 02 §4.3 exclusive-tract assignment (each shared catchment tract to its NEARER corridor); the harbor/streetcar pair shares 27.3% of the smaller catchment (near-threshold, computed anyway per the N1b review)
depth_cap 1 relabels the exploratory tail; objective invariant
depth_cap 3 relabels the exploratory tail; objective invariant

peak-mapped synthetic-feeder headway variant (spec 07 §4.2.1 G7 row: the injected committed line's {peak, offpeak} plan mapped to the feeder midday scalar via PEAK instead of the declared offpeak->midday convention)
per-line INDEPENDENT structural error N(0, sigma_struct=400 boardings) inflates the portfolio bands; P50 ~unchanged (mean-zero), the band widens (spec 07 §3/§8g)
fixed_cost_share 0.5 on lines 2..k (shared OCC+depot, §8j); Delta-K_PV display only, welfare objective invariant
fixed_cost_share 0.0 on lines 2..k (shared OCC+depot, §8j); Delta-K_PV display only, welfare objective invariant
harbor crossing count 2 (base 4, spec 04 §3.3 count uncertainty -- I-5/SR-22/Santa Ana River/BNSF Fullerton); Delta-K_PV display only, welfare objective invariant
harbor crossing count 6 (base 4, spec 04 §3.3 count uncertainty -- I-5/SR-22/Santa Ana River/BNSF Fullerton); Delta-K_PV display only, welfare objective invariant

named_spec_pending.note: order-difference diagnostic over the top-3 candidates (spec 07 §5.1)
R2 premium-bracket rows on every stop decision (§6.1)

stopping_record.bcr_rule_note: the BCR < 1 marginal stop is N5's NPV mode; the interim layer stops on budget exhaustion or candidate exhaustion and reports the economic margin at which it emptied (spec 07 §7).
stopping_record.cycle_numbering: 1-based: cycle 1 is the first evaluation, against the empty network-before (N5 review fix -- this field previously read 0-based, contradicting spec 07's own §7/§9 prose, which always calls the first evaluation 'cycle 1').
stopping_record.shadow_price_note: under a binding budget the marginal committed line's welfare margin is the shadow-price cutoff (spec 07 §7); it exceeds the null continuation, so the hurdle was not merely 0.
